w:r.w:t: The WebArt Technology Operations Platform is an internal business management system built for the WebArt Technology agency. It centralises sales submissions, QA auditing, client and project management, revenue tracking, meeting scheduling, and team administration into a single authenticated web application.
The platform replaces manual spreadsheet-based workflows and integrates with GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM via webhooks for real-time data sync and automated notifications.
Streamline the sales-to-delivery pipeline from deal submission to project delivery
Enforce QA auditing on every sale before a client project is created
Give the CEO real-time visibility into project health, revenue, and team performance
Automate alerts to GHL when projects are at risk or payments are received
Sync appointment bookings from GHL calendars into the platform
Main application — all views, routing, state
Public sales form at /sales (no login required)
Entry point — routes /sales to public form, else App
convertToUSD() with live API + fallback rates
Vercel serverless — GHL pushes meetings here
Edge function — invites new staff via service role
Server-side (Vercel env only — never exposed to browser):
service_role key from Supabase API settings

The platform uses Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) combined with role-based tab visibility in the React frontend. Every user has a role stored in the profiles table. The role is set at invite time and cannot be changed by the user.
Full access to everything. Receives at-risk alerts from GHL.
Full access same as CEO. Reviews and audits all sales submissions.
Dashboard, Clients, Projects, Revenue, Reactivation, Meetings.
Sale Submission form and Meetings only.

Staff are invited via the Team tab by CEO or QA. The invite flow uses the invite-staff Supabase Edge Function which calls auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail with the role and full_name stored in user metadata. When the invited user sets their password, the trg_on_new_auth_user trigger automatically creates their profile row.
All tables live in the public schema of the Supabase PostgreSQL database. RLS is enabled on all tables. UUID primary keys use uuid_generate_v4().
Linked to auth.users. One row per staff member. Auto-created on auth signup via trigger.
GHL booking page iframe embed code
Public URL from Supabase Storage avatars bucket
Key-value store for dynamic dropdown options used in forms. Updated via Form Config tab.
Website, SEO, Digital Marketing, SMO, App, Graphic, Others
Populated by CEO/QA via Form Config
Populated by CEO/QA via Form Config
USD, INR, GBP, AUD, CAD, EUR, AED, SGD
Every sale submitted by a sales rep. Status starts as pending and is updated to passed or failed when QA completes an audit.
USA | CANADA | UNITED KINGDOM | AUSTRALIA | INDIA
Notes from sales rep for QA auditor
Auto-created when QA passes a submission (via handle_qa_decision trigger). One client can have multiple projects.
Auto-created when QA passes a submission. Tracks the active work for each client deal. Health status is updated by the run_health_engine() function.
product / currency / gross / net / terms / sale_date
One row per completed audit. Inserting a row triggers handle_qa_decision() which creates client + project or marks submission failed.
PDF stored in Supabase Storage audit-reports bucket
Only set on Pass — assigns team to the new project
Communication logs added by AM/PM/CEO. Each insert triggers sync_last_communication() which updates projects.last_communication_date — this is the key date the health engine monitors.
Records all payment transactions. Amount is stored both in original currency and locked USD at the live exchange rate at time of entry. Once locked, the USD value cannot be changed.
Which project this payment is for
Live rate at time of entry
Synced from GHL via the api/sync-meetings.js Vercel endpoint. Uses upsert on ghl_meeting_id to handle updates and cancellations.
GHL appointment ID — used for upsert dedup
Staff member name — used for AM/PM filtering
Created when AM/PM requests re-engagement for a delivered client. Also updates the project status to reactivation.
Auto-creates profiles row from metadata (full_name, role)
On Pass: creates client + project. On Fail: marks submission failed.
Counts business days since last communication. Sets warning at 7d, critical at 15d. Returns rows where health status changed — used by frontend to fire GHL webhooks.
Counts Mon-Fri days between two dates (excludes weekends). Used by health engine and project_health view.
Returns role for the currently authenticated user. Used in RLS policies.
Returns full_name for the current user. Used in RLS policies for AM/PM data isolation.
Joins projects + clients. Includes business_days_inactive calculated column. Used everywhere projects are displayed.
Submissions filtered to status=pending, ordered by created_at ASC.
Aggregated totals — total_transactions, total_usd, collected_usd, outstanding_usd.
The CEO/QA/AM/PM dashboard shows 8 KPI cards, two bar charts, health alerts, and upcoming meetings.
submissions — sum of gross where status=passed
Charts: Sales by Month (deal count) and Sales by Rep (submission count) — both built with inline SVG-based bar chart component MiniBarChart.
Accessible to CEO, QA, and Sales roles. Also available as a public form at /sales (no login required) via SalesFormPublic.jsx.
Country selector with flag images (USA, Canada, UK, Australia, India)
Client contact fields: business name, client name, phone, email, website, address
Rep and Closer dropdowns — populated from app_settings
Product selector (icon grid) — populated from app_settings, "Others" always last
Currency, gross value, net value, payment terms, sale date
Sales Remarks field — visible to QA auditor during review
On submit: inserts to submissions table + fires sale_submission GHL webhook
Accessible to CEO and QA only.
shows cards for each unreviewed submission. Each card displays client name (prominent dark banner), business name, product, value, rep, closer, country, sale date, and sales remarks.
table view of all audited submissions with decision (Pass/Fail pill), auditor name, assigned AM/PM, and audit date.
filter by status (pending/passed/failed), rep/closer, and sale date range.

accessed by clicking "Launch Audit Review". Displays full submission details at top. Auditor enters QA date, score (10/10 down to Below 5), commitments verified (yes/no), notes, call recording URL, and uploads audit PDF to Supabase Storage. Decision is Pass or Fail. On Pass, assigns AM and PM from dropdowns.

handle_qa_decision trigger fires automatically. Pass: creates client row + project row, updates submission status to passed. Fail: updates submission status to failed. GHL webhook fired in both cases.
Runs every 5 minutes via setInterval in the App component (CEO, AM, PM roles only).
Function counts business days (Mon-Fri) since last_communication_date
7+ days inactive → health_status = warning, GHL webhook: project_health_warning
15+ days inactive → health_status = critical, project status → at_risk, GHL webhook: project_at_risk
Health only fires GHL webhook on status change (not every run)
Two tabs within the Revenue section:
logs all actual payments received. Currency, original amount, live exchange rate at entry, USD equivalent (locked). Total, collected, pending, and transaction count summary cards.

data from submissions table. Shows total submissions, passed count, total gross from passed sales, and conversion rate. Monthly volume chart, monthly gross chart, and rep performance breakdown table with pass rate percentage per rep.
Meeting data synced from GHL via Vercel webhook endpoint. Two tabs:
filter by status (scheduled/completed/cancelled/no_show/all). CEO/QA see all meetings. AM/PM/Sales see only meetings where assigned_to matches their full name.

CEO/QA see all active staff as tiles (excluding themselves). AM/PM/Sales see CEO tile + their own tile. Clicking a tile shows their GHL booking page iframe (750px height enforced). Each staff member adds their own iframe embed code via Profile settings.

The app uses window.location.hash for tab state. This means page refresh stays on the current tab. The hash is synced bidirectionally — setActiveTab() writes the hash, and a hashchange listener reads it. This also means back/forward browser buttons work.

All outbound webhooks are fired by triggerGHLWebhook(eventType, payload) in src/lib/ghl.js. The URL comes from VITE_GHL_WEBHOOK_URL environment variable. In GHL, create one Workflow with Inbound Webhook trigger and branch on the event_type field.
Send welcome email to client, notify AM/PM
Notify sales rep to follow up
Send reminder to AM/PM to log update
Create two GHL Workflows — one for Appointment Booked and one for Appointment Cancelled. Each workflow adds a Webhook action pointing to the endpoint above with the following JSON body:
The endpoint upserts on ghl_meeting_id so updates and cancellations overwrite the existing row rather than creating duplicates.
Built by Vite, served as static files
Every push to the main branch on GitHub automatically triggers a Vercel redeploy. No manual deployment steps required after initial setup.
Developer makes changes locally (npm run dev for local testing)
git add . && git commit -m "description" && git push
Vercel detects push, runs npm run build (Vite)
Build output (dist/) is deployed to Vercel CDN globally
For a new developer setting up locally:
Create .env file with the four VITE_ variables and the two server-side variables
Run dev server: npm run dev
Open http://localhost:5173 — log in with your staff account
The invite-staff edge function was deployed via the Supabase Dashboard (not CLI) due to Windows CLI compatibility issues. To redeploy or update:
Go to Supabase Dashboard → Edge Functions
Ensure JWT Verification is DISABLED for this function

The entire application is a single App.jsx file. Components are defined as named functions within the same file. This was an intentional choice to keep the codebase manageable for a small team. If the file grows significantly, extract components into src/components/.
Root — auth state, profile, settings, health engine, header, routing
Edit name, calendar iframe, upload avatar
8 KPI cards + 2 charts + health alerts + meetings
Full audit form with PDF upload
Filterable project list from project_health view
Project details + communication log + log update
Log a payment with live currency conversion
Invite staff, view team, revoke access
Log communication update with status change
Reusable components defined at the top of App.jsx:
Animated dot + days count + health pill
White bordered container with optional header
Overlay modal with backdrop click close
The app uses Supabase Realtime channels for live updates without page refresh:
app_settings — updates product/rep/closer dropdowns across all sessions instantly
submissions — updates QA queue count and pending cards in real-time
projects — updates project list and health indicators

AM and PM are assigned by name (string) not by user ID. If a staff member changes their name, project assignments will not auto-update. Consider migrating to assigned_am_id and assigned_pm_id UUID references in a future version.

The revenue Sales Analytics section reads gross values in their original currency without USD conversion. This means rep gross totals are multi-currency sums. A future version should convert all values to USD for accurate comparison.
The public /sales route (SalesFormPublic.jsx) uses the anon Supabase key with a permissive RLS policy allowing anon inserts to submissions. Monitor for spam submissions.
Currency conversion uses open.er-api.com free tier with a 1-hour in-memory cache. If the API is down, fallback rates hardcoded in currency.js are used.

The PageRouter was matching the string "audit-queue" as an audit form ID because it starts with "audit-". Fixed by adding && activeTab !== "audit-queue" guard to the route check. Do not remove this guard.
New tabs: add the tab to Nav (with role check), add a case to PageRouter switch, create the view function
New form dropdowns: add a new key to app_settings table and seed with default values, then add the key to the appSettings state default and the FormConfigView tabs array
New GHL webhook events: add a call to triggerGHLWebhook("your_event", payload) at the right point in the code, then add a branch in the GHL Workflow
New roles: add to the CHECK constraint on profiles.role, update Nav visibility conditions, update RLS policies
The SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must NEVER be exposed to the browser — it is server-side only in Vercel env vars and used only by api/sync-meetings.js and the Edge Function
.env is in .gitignore — never commit it to GitHub
All Supabase tables have RLS enabled — never disable RLS in production

The invite-staff Edge Function has JWT verification disabled because it is called from the browser with the anon key — this is intentional and safe because the function itself uses the service role key internally
git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push
SELECT id, full_name, email, role, status FROM profiles ORDER BY created_at;
SELECT id, client_name, business_name, product, gross, status FROM submissions WHERE status='pending';
SELECT client_name, status, health_status, business_days_inactive FROM project_health ORDER BY business_days_inactive DESC;
End of Document — WebArt Technology Operations Platform v1.0
